stimulus: Advertisement: Most nutritionists recommend eating fish twice a week. Eating tilapia fillets is a perfect choice for those who want the benefits of eating fish but do not care for the taste of fish. Tilapia fillets lack the strong fishy taste that many people find objectionable.
questionStem: Which one of the following, if true, most seriously weakens the advertisement’s argument?

answerChoices.A: Eating more than the recommended amount of fish can cause toxins that are present in high concentrations in many varieties of fish to accumulate in a person’s body.
answerChoices.B: Tilapia are invasive species that crowd out native species of fish in lakes throughout the world.
answerChoices.C: Tilapia fillets contain little of the beneficial fish oils that are the main reason nutritionists recommend eating fish frequently.
answerChoices.D: Most people who do not care for the taste of fish eat less fish than is recommended by most nutritionists.
answerChoices.E: People who rarely or never eat fish usually dislike any food with a strong fishy taste.

stimulus: Domestication of animals is a cooperative activity, and cooperative activities require a sophisticated means of communication. Language provides just such a means. It is likely, therefore, that language developed primarily to facilitate animal domestication.
questionStem: A flaw in the argument is that the argument

answerChoices.A: conflates being necessary for the development of a phenomenon with guaranteeing the development of that phenomenon
answerChoices.B: takes for granted that every phenomenon has a unique cause
answerChoices.C: infers that the development of one phenomenon caused the development of another merely because the two phenomena developed around the same time
answerChoices.D: draws a conclusion that merely restates a claim presented in support of that conclusion
answerChoices.E: assumes that if something serves a purpose it must have developed in order to serve that purpose

stimulus: Many employers treat their employees fairly. Thus, using others as a means to one’s own ends is not always morally reprehensible or harmful to others.
questionStem: The argument requires the assumption that

answerChoices.A: some employers act in a morally reprehensible manner only when they harm those whom they employ
answerChoices.B: no employers who act morally use their employees as a means to their own ends
answerChoices.C: some or all employers use their employees as a means to their own ends
answerChoices.D: making a profit from the labor of others is personally advantageous but never harmful
answerChoices.E: it is not possible to harm someone else without treating that person as a means to one’s own ends

stimulus: Editorial: It is common to find essays offering arguments that seem to show that our nation is in decline. There is no cause for alarm, however. The anxious tone of these essays shows that the problem is with the psychological state of their writers rather than with the actual condition of our nation.
questionStem: Which one of the following most accurately describes a flaw in the editorial’s reasoning?

answerChoices.A: The editorial dismisses a claim without considering any reasons presented in arguments for that claim.
answerChoices.B: The editorial compares two situations without considering the obvious differences between them.
answerChoices.C: The editorial confuses claims about a cultural decline with claims about a political decline.
answerChoices.D: The editorial overlooks the possibility that the nation is neither thriving nor in decline.
answerChoices.E: The editorial dismisses a particular view while offering evidence that actually supports that view.

stimulus: Eating turmeric, a spice commonly found in curries, probably helps prevent Alzheimer’s disease. More turmeric is consumed per capita in India than in the rest of the world, and the incidence of Alzheimer’s disease is much lower there than it is worldwide. Furthermore, Alzheimer’s disease is characterized by the buildup of amyloid protein plaques in the brain, and studies on animals found that curcumin—a compound found in turmeric—reduces the accumulation of amyloid proteins.
questionStem: Which one of the following, if true, most strengthens the argument?

answerChoices.A: Rosemary and ginger, which contain compounds that affect amyloid protein accumulation much like curcumin does, are commonly found in the diets of people living in India.
answerChoices.B: Many scientists believe that the buildup of amyloid protein plaques in the brain is a symptom of Alzheimer’s disease rather than a cause.
answerChoices.C: The proportion of people living in India who fall within the age group that is most prone to developing Alzheimer’s disease is smaller than the proportion of people worldwide who fall within that age group.
answerChoices.D: None of the other compounds found in turmeric have been studied to see whether they affect the accumulation of amyloid proteins.
answerChoices.E: The parts of India that have the highest per capita rates of curry consumption have the lowest incidence of Alzheimer’s disease.

stimulus: Forestry official: Many people think that if forest fires are not extinguished as quickly as possible, the Forestry Department is not doing its job properly. But relatively frequent, small fires clear out small trees and forest debris, which, if allowed to accumulate, would create the conditions for large, devastating fires. Therefore, it’s best to let small fires burn.
questionStem: The statement that relatively frequent, small fires clear out small trees and forest debris plays which one of the following roles in the official’s argument?

answerChoices.A: It is offered as support for the contention that the Forestry Department is not doing its job properly if it does not extinguish forest fires as quickly as possible.
answerChoices.B: It is used as evidence against the contention that the Forestry Department is not doing its job properly if it does not extinguish forest fires as quickly as possible.
answerChoices.C: It is used to show what the consequences would be if the Forestry Department based its policies on the ideas most people have about how it should do its job.
answerChoices.D: It is an example used to illustrate the claim that most people believe the Forestry Department should quickly extinguish all forest fires.
answerChoices.E: It is a conclusion based on the premise in the argument that it is best to let small forest fires burn.

stimulus:
Gerald: Unless a consumer secures his or her home wireless Internet service, anyone strolling by is able to access that person’s service with certain laptop computers or smartphones. Such use cannot be considered illegal under current laws: it’s no more like trespassing than is enjoying music playing on someone’s radio as you walk down the street.
Kendra: But unlike hearing music while walking by, accessing wireless service requires stopping for a considerable length of time. And that could be considered loitering or even harassment.
questionStem: Gerald’s and Kendra’s positions indicate that they disagree over whether accessing someone’s wireless Internet service while walking down the street

answerChoices.A: can be considered illegal under current law
answerChoices.B: is like trespassing
answerChoices.C: should be prohibited by law
answerChoices.D: requires a considerable length of time
answerChoices.E: could be done without intending to do so

stimulus: Over the last thousand years, plant species native to islands have gone extinct at a much faster rate than have those native to mainland regions. Biologists believe that this is because island plants have not adapted the defenses against being eaten by large land mammals that mainland plants have. Ordinarily, populations of large land mammals are not established on islands until after the island is colonized by humans.
questionStem: Which one of the following, if true, most strongly supports the biologist’s explanation cited above?

answerChoices.A: Most of the plant species in the world that have not yet gone extinct are native to mainland regions.
answerChoices.B: Many plant species that are not native to islands have become very well established on islands throughout the world.
answerChoices.C: Commercial development on many islands has resulted in loss of habitat for many native plants.
answerChoices.D: The rate of extinction of native plant species on an island tends to increase dramatically after human colonization.
answerChoices.E: Large land mammals tend to prefer plants from species native to mainland regions over plants from species native to islands.

stimulus: As regards memory, the brain responds best to repeated patterns, such as the melodic and rhythmic patterns of music. This is why we can remember long strings of information or text, which would normally be impossible to memorize, when they are put to music. Given that music aids memory, it might seem that funny jokes would be easy to remember, since, like music, they normally elicit an emotional response in us. However, jokes are usually very difficult to remember, since _______.
questionStem: Which one of the following most logically completes the passage?

answerChoices.A: jokes, unlike music, always have content that is verbal or at least clearly symbolic
answerChoices.B: some successful jokes are short and pithy, whereas others are long and involved
answerChoices.C: jokes work not by conforming to repeated patterns but by breaking them
answerChoices.D: for most people, certain memories elicit a strong emotional response
answerChoices.E: people can hold in short-term memory only a few chunks of unpatterned information at a time

stimulus: The prehistoric fish Tiktaalik is the earliest known animal with fingers. Since variations were so great among prehistoric fish species, Tiktaalik would not have stood out as unusual at the time. However, Tiktaalik’s fingers were an important development in animal evolution because it is likely that Tiktaalik is an ancestor to the many land animals with fingers.
questionStem: The statements above, if true, most strongly support which one of the following?

answerChoices.A: Tiktaalik likely used its fingers to move on land.
answerChoices.B: Tiktaalik’s fingers were its only feature to play a significant role in the development of modern land animals.
answerChoices.C: Tiktaalik is not the ancestor of any currently surviving fish species.
answerChoices.D: No fish without fingers would ever be able to move on land.
answerChoices.E: The evolutionary significance of Tiktaalik could not be determined just through comparison to fish species of its time.

stimulus:
Gabriella: By raising interest rates, the government has induced people to borrow less money and therefore to spend less, thereby slowing the country’s economy.
Ivan: I disagree with your analysis. The country’s economy is tied to the global economy. Whatever happens to the global economy also happens here, and the global economy has slowed. Therefore, the government’s action did not cause the economy’s slowdown.
questionStem: Gabriella and Ivan disagree about whether

answerChoices.A: the economic slowdown in the country has caused people to spend less
answerChoices.B: the economy of the country is tied to the economies of other countries
answerChoices.C: raising interest rates caused a significant decrease in borrowing
answerChoices.D: raising interest rates caused the country’s economy to slow
answerChoices.E: the global economy has slowed

stimulus: In a scene in an ancient Greek play, Knights, the character Demosthenes opens a writing tablet on which an oracle had written a prophecy, and while looking at the tablet, he continuously expresses his amazement at its contents. His companion presses him for information, whereupon Demosthenes explains what the oracle had written.
questionStem: Of the following claims, which one can most justifiably be rejected on the basis of the statements above?

answerChoices.A: In ancient Greek plays, characters are presumed to know how to read unless their illiteracy is specifically mentioned.
answerChoices.B: The character of Demosthenes in Knights is not based on a historical figure.
answerChoices.C: In ancient Greek plays, the reading aloud of written texts commonly occurred as part of the on-stage action.
answerChoices.D: In ancient Greece, people did not read silently to themselves.
answerChoices.E: Only rarely in ancient Greece were prophecies written down on writing tablets.

stimulus: Science cannot adequately explain emotional phenomena such as feeling frustrated, falling in love, or being moved by a painting. Since they cannot be explained by physics, chemistry, or neurophysiology, human emotions must not be physical phenomena.
questionStem: The conclusion follows logically if which one of the following is assumed?

answerChoices.A: Whatever is not a physical phenomenon cannot be explained by science.
answerChoices.B: Nothing that can be felt by only one subject can be studied scientifically.
answerChoices.C: Physics, chemistry, and neurophysiology have similar explanatory frameworks.
answerChoices.D: Whatever is not a physical phenomenon is an emotional one.
answerChoices.E: Every physical phenomenon can be explained by physics, chemistry, or neurophysiology.

stimulus: Several Tyrannosaurus rex skeletons found in North America contain tooth marks that only a large carnivore could have made. At the time T. rex lived, it was the only large carnivore in North America. The tooth marks could have resulted only from combat or feeding. But such tooth marks would have been almost impossible to inflict on the skeleton of a live animal.
questionStem: The information above most strongly supports which one of the following?

answerChoices.A: T. rex regularly engaged in combat with smaller carnivores.
answerChoices.B: At the time T. rex lived, it was common for carnivores to feed on other carnivores.
answerChoices.C: T. rex sometimes engaged in cannibalism.
answerChoices.D: T. rex sometimes engaged in intraspecies combat.
answerChoices.E: At the time T. rex lived, there were large carnivores on continents other than North America.

stimulus: There is a popular view among literary critics that a poem can never be accurately paraphrased because a poem is itself the only accurate expression of its meaning. But these same critics hold that their own paraphrases of particular poems are accurate. Thus, their view that poetry cannot be accurately paraphrased is false.
questionStem: The reasoning in the argument is flawed because the argument

answerChoices.A: presupposes the falsity of the view that it sets out to refute
answerChoices.B: takes for granted that the main purpose of poems is to convey information rather than express feelings
answerChoices.C: takes for granted that a paraphrase of a poem cannot be useful to its readers unless it accurately expresses a poem’s meaning
answerChoices.D: provides no justification for favoring one of the literary critics’ beliefs over the other
answerChoices.E: provides no justification for following one particular definition of “paraphrase”

stimulus: The tax bill passed 2 years ago provides substantial incentives for businesses that move to this area and hire 50 or more employees. Critics say the bill reduces the government’s tax revenues. Yet clearly it has already created many jobs in this area. Last year, Plastonica qualified for incentives under the bill by opening a new plastics factory here that hired 75 employees.
questionStem: The argument’s reasoning depends on which one of the following assumptions?

answerChoices.A: If Plastonica had not opened the plastics factory in the area, it would not have opened a plastics factory at all.
answerChoices.B: Plastonica would not have opened the plastics factory in the area had it not been for the incentives.
answerChoices.C: Most critics of the tax bill claim that it will not create any more new jobs.
answerChoices.D: If Plastonica had not opened the plastics factory in the area, it would have opened it somewhere else.
answerChoices.E: Critics of the tax bill believe that it has not created any jobs in the area.

stimulus: When a chain of service stations began applying a surcharge of $0.25 per purchase on fuel paid for by credit card, the chain’s owners found that this policy made their customers angry. So they decided instead to simply raise the price of fuel a compensatory amount and give a $0.25 discount to customers paying with cash. Customers were much happier with this policy.
questionStem: Which one of the following generalizations does the situation described above most clearly illustrate?

answerChoices.A: People usually adopt beliefs without carefully assessing the evidence for and against those beliefs.
answerChoices.B: People’s perceptions of the fairness of a policy sometimes depend on whether that policy benefits them personally.
answerChoices.C: People usually become emotional when considering financial issues.
answerChoices.D: People often change their minds about issues that do not make significant differences to their lives.
answerChoices.E: People’s evaluations of a situation sometimes depend less on the situation itself than on how it is presented to them.

stimulus: Herbalist: Many herbal medicines work best when they have a chance to influence the body gently over several months. However, many of these herbal medicines have toxic side effects when taken daily for such long periods. Therefore, at least some people who use herbal medicines daily should occasionally skip their usual dose for a day or two, to give the body a chance to recuperate.
questionStem: Which one of the following is an assumption required by the herbalist’s argument?

answerChoices.A: At least some people who use herbal medicines daily use them for periods long enough for the medicines to have side effects.
answerChoices.B: At least some herbal medicines work less well in achieving their desired effects if one occasionally skips one’s usual dose than if one does not.
answerChoices.C: Some herbal medicines have toxic side effects when taken for several months, even if the usual dose is occasionally skipped for a day or two to give the body a chance to recuperate.
answerChoices.D: Anyone who uses herbal medicines should give those medicines a chance to influence the body gently over several months at least.
answerChoices.E: One should occasionally skip one’s usual dose of an herbal medicine for a day or two only if doing so will reduce or eliminate toxic side effects from several months of use.

stimulus:
Ana: On libertarian principles, I oppose the proposed smoking ban. It is not the government’s business to prevent people from doing things that harm only themselves.
Pankaj: But keep in mind that the ban would apply only to smoking in public places. People could still smoke all they want in private.
questionStem: The dialogue provides the most support for the claim that Ana and Pankaj disagree over whether

answerChoices.A: it is the government’s business to prevent people from harming themselves
answerChoices.B: government should be restrained by libertarian principles
answerChoices.C: the proposed smoking ban is intended to prevent harm only to smokers themselves
answerChoices.D: the proposed ban would prohibit smoking in public places
answerChoices.E: there are cases in which government should attempt to regulate private behavior

stimulus: Agricultural scientist: Wild apples are considerably smaller than cultivated apples found in supermarkets. In one particular region, archaeologists have looked for remains of cultivated apples dating from 5,000 years ago, around the time people first started cultivating fruit. But the only remains of apples that archaeologists have found from this period are from fruits the same size as the wild apples native to the region. So apples were probably not cultivated in this region 5,000 years ago.
questionStem: The agricultural scientist’s argument is most vulnerable to criticism on the grounds that the argument

answerChoices.A: fails to consider that even if a plant was not cultivated in a given region at a specific time, it may have been cultivated in nearby regions at that time
answerChoices.B: fails to consider that plants that have been cultivated for only a short time may tend to resemble their wild counterparts much more closely than plants that have been cultivated for a long time
answerChoices.C: takes for granted that all apples are either the size of wild apples or the size of the cultivated apples now found in supermarkets
answerChoices.D: employs a premise that is incompatible with the conclusion it is supposed to justify
answerChoices.E: uses a claim that presupposes the truth of its main conclusion as part of the justification for that conclusion

stimulus: Genuine happiness consists not in pleasurable feelings but instead in one’s sense of approval of one’s character and projects. Thus the happy life, in fact, tends to be the good life, where the good life is understood not—as it usually is these days—as a life of material well-being but rather as a morally virtuous life.
questionStem: Which one of the following is an assumption required by the argument?

answerChoices.A: A morally virtuous life requires the rejection of material well-being.
answerChoices.B: People who approve of their own character and projects tend to lead morally virtuous lives.
answerChoices.C: Approval of one’s own character and projects tends not to result in pleasurable feelings.
answerChoices.D: Attaining happiness is the real goal of people who strive for material well-being.
answerChoices.E: Material well-being does not increase one’s sense of approval of one’s character and projects.

stimulus: The return of organic wastes to the soil is a good solution to waste disposal problems only if the wastes are nontoxic and not too much energy is expended in transporting them. In small-scale organic farming, the wastes are nontoxic and not too much energy is expended in transporting them. Hence, returning organic wastes to the soil is a good way for small-scale organic farms to solve their waste disposal problems.
questionStem: Which one of the following exhibits flawed reasoning most similar to the flawed reasoning exhibited by the argument above?

answerChoices.A: Plants thrive if they get an abundance of moisture, light, and nutrients. In greenhouses, plants get an optimal combination of all three, which is why commercially produced plants are so healthy when you first buy them.
answerChoices.B: When every country has equal access to markets, which will be the case 20 years from now, globalization of markets will provide a way for each country to optimize its use of resources. So, globalization of markets will show the desired results 20 years from now.
answerChoices.C: To be viable, a business idea must be clear, cost-effective, practical, and responsive to a market demand. Your idea for a website information service has all these properties, so it is viable.
answerChoices.D: Those competitors—and only those—who meet all of the following criteria are eligible for the award: they must be under 19 years of age, be in secondary school, and have played the sport for at least the two years immediately preceding the competition. You meet all the criteria, so you are eligible.
answerChoices.E: A meal is nutritious only if it includes both carbohydrates and protein. Almost 80 percent of the calories in what I ate for lunch were from fat, so what I ate for lunch was not nutritious.

stimulus: Scientist: Some colonies of bacteria produce antibiotic molecules called phenazines, which they use to fend off other bacteria. We hypothesize that phenazines also serve as molecular pipelines that give interior bacteria access to essential nutrients in the environment surrounding the colony.
questionStem: Which one of the following, if true, provides the most support for the scientist’s hypothesis?

answerChoices.A: Bacteria colonies that do not produce phenazines form wrinkled surfaces, thus increasing the number of bacteria that are in direct contact with the surrounding environment.
answerChoices.B: The rate at which a bacteria colony produces phenazines is determined by the number of foreign bacteria in the environment immediately surrounding the colony.
answerChoices.C: When bacteria colonies that do not produce phenazines are buried in nutrient-rich soil, they grow as quickly as colonies that do produce phenazines.
answerChoices.D: Bacteria colonies that produce phenazines are better able to fend off other bacteria than are bacteria colonies that do not produce phenazines.
answerChoices.E: Within bacteria colonies that produce phenazines, interior bacteria are more likely to die than are bacteria along the edges.

stimulus: Library preservationist: Due to the continual physical deterioration of the medieval manuscripts in our library’s collection, we have decided to restore most of our medieval manuscripts that are of widely acknowledged cultural significance, though this means that some medieval manuscripts whose authenticity is suspect will be restored. However, only manuscripts whose safety can be ensured during the restoration process will be restored, and manuscripts that are not frequently consulted by researchers will not be restored.
questionStem: If all of the library preservationist’s statements are true, which one of the following must be true of the medieval manuscripts in the library’s collection?

answerChoices.A: Some of the medieval manuscripts whose authenticity is suspect are frequently consulted by researchers.
answerChoices.B: All of the medieval manuscripts widely acknowledged to be of cultural significance are manuscripts whose safety can be ensured during the restoration process.
answerChoices.C: All of the medieval manuscripts whose safety can be ensured during the restoration process are frequently consulted by researchers.
answerChoices.D: The medieval manuscripts most susceptible to deterioration are those most frequently consulted by researchers.
answerChoices.E: None of the medieval manuscripts that are rarely consulted by researchers is widely acknowledged to be of cultural significance.

stimulus: Direct-mail advertising usually consists of advertisements for products to be purchased from the home, so the perception that it is bad for the environment is misguided. Because of direct-mail advertising, millions of people buy products by phone or online—products whose purchase would otherwise require the use of a car, thus adding pollutants to the air.
questionStem: Which one of the following, if true, would most strengthen the argument?

answerChoices.A: Although the primary intent of most direct-mail advertisers is to convince people to buy products from their homes, direct mail can also lead to increased sales in stores by customers who prefer to see a product prior to purchasing it.
answerChoices.B: Most of the products purchased in response to direct-mail advertisements would be purchased even without the direct-mail advertisements.
answerChoices.C: A person who receives and reads a direct-mail advertisement is more likely to purchase the product advertised than is a person who reads an advertisement for a product in a magazine that they subscribe to.
answerChoices.D: Usually, a company that sends out direct-mail advertisements has good reason to think that the person to whom the advertisement is sent would be more interested in the product than would the average person.
answerChoices.E: Products purchased as the result of direct-mail advertising comprise an increasingly large portion of the consumer products purchased each year.

stimulus: The older a country is, the more likely it is to be ruled by a monarch. Thus, since most countries are not ruled by monarchs, if a country is particularly new it is probably not ruled by a monarch.
questionStem: The pattern of reasoning in the argument above is most similar to that in which one of the following arguments?

answerChoices.A: Most novels are not made into movies. However, the more popular a novel is, the more likely it is to be made into a movie. Thus, if a movie is quite unpopular, it was probably not based on a novel.
answerChoices.B: Most novels are not made into movies. However, the more popular a movie is, the more likely it is that the movie was based on a novel. Thus, if a novel is particularly popular, it will probably be made into a movie.
answerChoices.C: Most novels are not made into movies. Moreover, if a novel is particularly unpopular, it will probably not be made into a movie. Thus, the more popular a novel is, the more likely it is to be made into a movie.
answerChoices.D: Most novels are not made into movies. However, the more popular a novel is, the more likely it is to be made into a movie. Thus, if a novel is quite unpopular, it will probably not be made into a movie.
answerChoices.E: Most novels are not made into movies. Moreover, the more complex a novel’s plot, the less likely the novel is to be made into a movie. Thus, if a novel has a particularly simple plot, it will probably be made into a movie.
